message:
test(fcp-e2e): fix + strengthen notion/linear/youtube allow paths (br-7e15h, br-1maun-alikes)

Three compliance tests were failing at the existing allow-path
assertion for the same two reasons telegram was (br-1maun):

  1. build_token helper passed the operation id as the capability
     arg. Each connector's introspection declares its operations
     require a permission *class* capability (e.g. `notion.read`),
     not the op id (`notion.get_page`). After br-8n0rm.6 removed
     the legacy OPERATIONS fallback, the strict GRANTS check at
     capability.rs:2001-2013 sees `g.capability != required_capability`
     and emits OperationNotGranted (FCP-3003).
  2. Missing `constraints` claim. C3.4 default-deny at
     capability.rs:2017-2029 rejects tokens with no constraints.

Per-connector op→capability corrections:
  * notion: get_page   → read  (was: get_page)
  * linear: get_issue  → read  (was: get_issue)
  * youtube: get_video → read  (was: get_video)

All three build_token helpers now attach a wildcard
`CapabilityConstraints { resource_allow: ["*"] }` via
constraints_cbor, matching the pattern landed for telegram in
d96120d0.

Also added br-7e15h mock-hit assertions — the original motivation
for the audit that surfaced 1maun. Each test now independently
verifies the connector actually hit the mock HTTP server, closing
the "passed-but-never-called" gap E2eRunner can't catch:

  * notion: mock.assert_received("/pages/<id>")
  * linear: mock.assert_received("/graphql")
  * youtube: received_requests filter for GET /videos* (client
    appends a query string, so path prefix + method check)

Default-deny tests were intentionally left alone in each file —
their observable behavior (denial expected) is unchanged by the
capability/operation correction; denial still happens regardless.

Verified per-crate:
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-review-A cargo test -p fcp-e2e \
    --no-default-features --features {notion,linear,youtube} \
    --test {notion,linear,youtube}_compliance_e2e
→ 9 passed, 0 failed (3 tests per connector).

Follow-up noted: asana, airtable, bitbucket, clickup, datadog,
dropbox, elasticsearch still fail to COMPILE with their own
features — pre-existing drift unrelated to the 1maun class.
Filing a separate bead for those once they're triaged.

Part of br-7e15h. No assertions weakened anywhere.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
